alignments.candidate_evidence: SQL proficient with joins, GROUP BY, subqueries/CTEs, window functions; BigQuery querying; Python basic, actively learning (data-analysis/pandas track)
alignments.job_requirement: Proficient in programming, debugging, and problem-solving using SQL and Python

alignments.candidate_evidence: Eight years at Google Devices and Services; no explicit cloud architecture, deployment, or solution-building experience documented
alignments.job_requirement: Hands-on experience building solutions within major public cloud environments (AWS, Azure, or GCP)

alignments.candidate_evidence: Commercial operations and transformation programs at Google; no documented GSI/consulting partner collaboration
alignments.job_requirement: Experience collaborating with Global System Integrators (GSIs) and third-party consulting organizations to drive customer outcomes

alignments.candidate_evidence: Eight-year tenure with two promotions scaling global commercial operations; demonstrated stakeholder influence and GTM leadership
alignments.job_requirement: Ability to influence technical executives and drive security strategy and product adoption

alignments.candidate_evidence: No documented security operations, SOC, SIEM, detection engineering, or cybersecurity background; AI/agentic tooling design and direction in non-security context
alignments.job_requirement: Deep experience in security operations, data engineering, data warehousing, AI/ML for security, data governance, or streaming

alignments.candidate_evidence: Designs and directs LLM coding agents; no documented anomaly detection, behavioral analytics, security-domain agentic workflows
alignments.job_requirement: Experience with design and implementation of data and AI applications including anomaly detection, behavioral analytics, agentic AI workflows

gaps.gap: Cybersecurity domain expertise and SecOps/security operations background; no SIEM, detection engineering, EDR/XDR, identity, or telemetry landscape experience
gaps.mitigation: Candidate would require 12–18 months of intensive security-domain immersion, mentorship from security architects, and hands-on SIEM tuning/deployment to credibly advise CISOs and SOC teams. Unlikely to close at this role's tier-3 escalation scope.

gaps.gap: Limited customer-facing pre-sales, consulting, or solutions architecture experience in technical engagements; background is commercial operations and transformation rather than technical positioning and POC delivery
gaps.mitigation: Requires direct mentorship in technical discovery, RFP response, POC scoping, and security-specific customer advisory patterns. Timeline to independent contribution is 9–12 months.

gaps.gap: No demonstrated experience with cloud-native security logging, public-cloud architecture, or hands-on solution deployment on AWS/Azure/GCP
gaps.mitigation: Foundational cloud learning is accessible; however, security-specific cloud telemetry (CloudTrail, Activity Log, Audit logs) and SIEM migration strategies require domain context not in profile.

gaps.gap: Python remains at basic/learning level; role requires proficiency in debugging and production problem-solving with Python
gaps.mitigation: Python can be upskilled to intermediate in 3–6 months; senior SQL and agentic-AI-tool direction provide partial mitigation, but depth in Python-based anomaly detection and triage workflows is missing.

gaps.gap: No GSI or third-party consulting partner collaboration experience; no demonstrated ability to orchestrate complex multi-party security transformation engagements
gaps.mitigation: Partner collaboration models are learnable through mentoring and initial engagements; not a core blocker but requires ramp.

output.level_rationale: JD requires 5+ years customer-facing pre-sales/consulting in security, with deep SecOps, SIEM, detection engineering, and security-product roadmap influence. Tier-3 technical escalation scope and CISO/SOC advisory role point to L5 IC with established security-domain credibility. No managing scope documented. role maps to L5 seniority floor in Databricks field-solutions hierarchy.
output.summary: This is a security-specialized Solutions Architect role requiring 5+ years of SecOps, SIEM, and detection-engineering expertise with deep customer-facing consulting and pre-sales credentials. Candidate has strong GTM and stakeholder-influence background but no cybersecurity domain experience, SIEM/SOC knowledge, detection-engineering expertise, or hands-on security-solutions delivery. Gap is not upskillable within realistic ramp timeline; role demands tier-3 escalation credibility with CISOs and security architects from day one. Function fit is poor; seniority and leadership capability alone do not close domain and customer-facing technical gaps.
uncertainties: Candidate's full experience with data-engineering and cloud-architecture depth at Google Devices is not fully documented; unknown whether prior roles included security-domain application experience
Extent of candidate's hands-on security-analytics background beyond commercial operations not specified in profile
Timeline and appetite for domain pivot from commercial operations to specialized cybersecurity pre-sales unknown
